studyName: Epidemiologic Architecture for Genes Linked to Environment (EAGLE) MetaboChip Study

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes demographic information (n=3 variables; race, gender, age), anthropometric information (n=3 variables; weight, height, BMI), medication status relative to lab values (n=4 variables; total cholesterol, triglycerides, LDL, HDL), age associated with lab value (n=4 variables; total cholesterol, triglycerides, LDL, HDL), first mention of lab value in electronic records (n=4 variables; total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides), number of lipid medications mentioned in electronic records, type of lipid drug mentioned, and smoking status estimated from health records.

variables.name: BMI_CALCULATED
variables.description: Body mass index calcuated from extracted weight and height

variables.name: GENDER
variables.description: Gender extracted from electronic health records

variables.name: HDL
variables.description: First mention of subject's high-density lipoprotein cholesterol lab value in the electronic health record

variables.name: HDL_AGE
variables.description: Subject's age associated with high-density lipoprotein cholesterol lab value

variables.name: HDL_MEDICATION
variables.description: Subject's lipid-lowering medication status relative to high-density lipoprotein cholesterol lab value extracted

variables.name: HEIGHT
variables.description: Height extracted

variables.name: LDL
variables.description: First mention of subject's low density lipoprotein cholesterol lab value in the electronic health record

variables.name: LDL_AGE
variables.description: Subject's age associated with low density lipoprotein cholesterol lab value

variables.name: LDL_MEDICATION
variables.description: Subject's lipid-lowering medication status relative to low density lipoprotein cholesterol lab value extracted

variables.name: LIPID_DRUGS
variables.description: Subject's number of lipid lowering medications mentioned in the electronic health record

variables.name: LIPID_DRUG_CLASS
variables.description: Type of lipid drug mentioned

variables.name: RACE
variables.description: Administratively reported race in electronic health records

variables.name: SMOKER_STATUS
variables.description: Smoking status estimated from electronic health records

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: TOTAL_CHOLESTEROL
variables.description: First mention of subject's total cholesterol lab value in the electronic health record

variables.name: TOTAL_CHOLESTEROL_AGE
variables.description: Subject's age associated with total cholesterol lab value

variables.name: TOTAL_CHOLESTEROL_MEDICATION
variables.description: Subject's lipid-lowering medication status relative to total cholesterol lab value extracted

variables.name: TRIGLYCERIDES
variables.description: First mention of subject's triglycerides lab value in the electronic health record

variables.name: TRIGLYCERIDES_AGE
variables.description: Subject's age associated with triglycerides lab value

variables.name: TRIGLYCERIDES_MEDICATION
variables.description: Subject's lipid-lowering medication status relative to triglyceride lab value extracted

variables.name: WEIGHT
variables.description: Weight extracted

variables.name: WEIGHT_HEIGHT_AGE
variables.description: Age at extracted weight and height extracted from electronic health records

tables.description: The subject consent dataset contains subject IDs, consent group information, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Biological sex

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes body site and analyte type.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
